methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Ark Robotics is a seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of five same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables, spanning both seed and Series A companies. The peer set ranges from Tenna Systems at $60M (seed) to Scale AI at $29.0B (Series A), with intermediate benchmarks including Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given available data. The median valuation across these five comparables is $300M, establishing a central reference point. This method captures the distribution of capital deployed in comparable Defense & Security investments at overlapping stages, though the wide dispersion (60x spread between floor and ceiling) reflects heterogeneity in company maturity and market positioning within the peer cohort.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, centered on $300M. This range reflects defensible peer benchmarking but carries material uncertainty given the single-method foundation and the substantial variance across the comparable set. Key uncertainties include Ark's specific technology differentiation relative to peers, traction metrics relative to the comparison set, and investor composition or follow-on capacity signals. The inclusion of high-outlier Scale AI ($29.0B) underscores that peer-based valuation in this sector remains sensitive to outlier outcomes and stage progression.
